title: haraella retrocalla
intro: Haraella retrocalla, a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, is native to New Guinea. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where its extracts have been used to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Haraella retrocalla are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, wounds, and skin conditions, thereby providing health benefits such as improved wound healing and reduced inflammation. Haraella retrocalla can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
